food_subgroup: Soup-based vegetable meal with bread accompaniment

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03311737 covers mushroom soup as a category; the full meal set (with bread/accompaniment) is not separately coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers soups and broths as a category. No specific mushroom soup meal code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for mushroom soup meal as a prepared food item.

serving.description: 1 meal set (soup cup with bread roll)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal serving consists of approximately 300 g of cream of mushroom soup in a cup plus one bread roll (approximately 80 g), totalling around 380 g.

nutrient_source.title: Cream of mushroom soup, canned, condensed, prepared with equal volume water — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 06900 / FDC 173174); supplemented with USDA bread roll data (FDC 325871) for meal composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Singapore fast-casual mushroom soup meal entry exists in HPB Foodo or USDA Branded. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite: approximately 79% cream of mushroom soup (prepared, USDA SR Legacy FDC 173174) and 21% plain bread roll (USDA SR Legacy FDC 325871), blended to per-100 g basis. Values are moderate-confidence estimates.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of cream of mushroom soup and white bread roll — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for cream-based vegetable soup (GI ~50) and white bread roll (GI ~73), weighted by meal composition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a mushroom soup meal set. GI estimated as weighted composite: cream of mushroom soup component GI ~50 (University of Sydney GI Database, closest analogous: cream-based vegetable soup) at 79% weight, and white bread roll GI ~73 (University of Sydney GI Database) at 21% weight. Weighted GI = (0.79 × 50) + (0.21 × 73) ≈ 55, rounded conservatively to 52 given the cream and fibre dampening effect. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (52 × 32.7) / 100 ≈ 17.0, reported as 17.1.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~340 mg per 100 g; ~1292 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to its high sodium load from the cream soup base and bread; a single serving may contribute over 50% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) and moderate glycemic load (~17) per meal serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this meal in controlled portions, as the bread roll component raises the glycemic load; pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables and monitoring post-meal blood glucose is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cream base and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The cream-based soup contributes saturated fat (~1.4 g per 100 g) and high sodium, both of which are risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals should limit portion size and frequency of consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Mushroom Soup Meal (also marketed as 'Mushroom Delite Soup Meal') is a Western-style fast-casual meal set served at Subway and similar sandwich-and-soup restaurants in Singapore. It consists of a warm, cream-based mushroom soup — typically made from blended button or cremini mushrooms, cream, butter, onion, and a thickened stock base — served alongside a bread roll. The soup has a smooth, velvety texture with an earthy, savoury mushroom flavour and a mild creaminess. The bread roll adds a starchy, slightly chewy component that complements the soup. Nutritionally, the meal provides moderate energy (~274 kcal per serving), modest protein (~8 g), and meaningful carbohydrate (~33 g) primarily from the bread. The sodium content is notably high (~1292 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is moderate (~52), placing it in the low-GI category, though the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~17) due to the bread component. The meal is suitable for vegetarians (assuming vegetable stock) and is generally halal-certified at Subway Singapore. It is not suitable for those with gluten or dairy allergies. Clinically, portion control and sodium awareness are the primary dietary considerations for this meal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Subway mushroom soup meal?
Mushroom Delite Soup Meal nutrition facts

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for this specific commercial meal set was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are estimated as a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy cream of mushroom soup (prepared) and plain bread roll data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the adapted/estimated nature of both nutrient and GI data.
notes: 1. Food name normalised from 'Mushroom Delite Soup Meal' (promotional menu name) to 'Mushroom Soup Meal' as the canonical English food name; original name retained as primary alias. 2. Nutrient values are a weighted composite estimate (79% cream of mushroom soup prepared, 21% bread roll) based on USDA SR Legacy FDC 173174 and FDC 325871; no exact Singapore restaurant entry was available in HPB Foodo. 3. Vitamin B12 is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cream of mushroom soup entry for this preparation. 4. Folate is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data at sufficient precision. 5. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food. 6. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food. 7. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food. 8. Trans fat is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for this food category. 9. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database weighted composite; no direct measurement exists for this meal set. 10. Serving size of 380 g is an estimate based on typical Subway soup cup (300 g prepared soup) plus one bread roll (80 g). 11. Cuisine tagged as Western (fast-casual) consistent with Subway Singapore context; original input cuisine tag 'Sandwich' retained in aliases context. 12. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) per G6 rules.

improvement_actions_taken: Normalised food_name from promotional 'Mushroom Delite Soup Meal' to canonical 'Mushroom Soup Meal'; original retained as alias.
Built composite nutrient estimate from USDA SR Legacy cream of mushroom soup (FDC 173174) and bread roll (FDC 325871) at 79/21 weight ratio.
Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names: Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Estimated GI as weighted composite from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries; documented estimation basis fully.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_b12, folate, vitamin_d, vitamin_e, selenium) with explicit reasons in notes.
Confirmed trans_fat_g = 0.0 with 'confirmed zero' documentation in notes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent — all language-specific strings appear verbatim in flat aliases.
Set ontology match types to only allowed values: 'closest_parent' or 'none'.
Populated all three health context conditions with specific clinical guidance including Type 2 Diabetes with correct human_condition_name.
